rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICK EDWARD NASH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) The Public Administrator retained $45,000 after the general administrator was appointed as a reserve for their statutory fees, without court authority. Public Administrator states that they have taken similar actions previously in other cases without question. 2) The Public Administrator distributed assets to "Estate of Patrick Edward Nash, c/o Thomas Reed", which allowed the amount to be deposited into the attorney's client trust account (see 12/10/19 supplemental declaration and back of cancelled check). 3) Petitioner alleges that all cash in the estate has been held in an interest-bearing account; however, the accounting shows $1.2M in cash at the end of the account but no accrued interest during the accounting period. Remote appearances are highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 6th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGUIRE REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 11/16/92 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Both trustee and attorney must appear. Issue: Trustee continues to fail to account after two continuances. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VISHWAS A. NARURKAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication (Added To Calendar For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: Part 2 of 2 The drafting attorneys were not named or referenced in the April 29, 2019 Will Contest. The pleadings define the scope of the issues on a motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication. (Jacobs v Coldwell Banker Residential Brokerage Co. (2017) 14 CA5th 438, 444-446.) The issue of whether the drafting attorneys exercised undue influence, duress or menace over decedent in procuring the challenge testamentary instrument is outside the scope of the pleadings, and thus outside of the scope of this motion. Likewise, fraud in procuring the challenged testamentary instrument is not pleaded in the Will Contest. It is outside the scope of the pleadings, and thus outside the scope of this motion. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3772) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 1, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. Part 2 of 2

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VISHWAS A. NARURKAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Part 1 of 2 The Court grants Petitioner's request for judicial notice as to the existence of the specified court records pursuant to Evidence Code section 452, but not to the truth of any matter asserted in these documents. Any reply to the papers filed in opposition to the motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication must be served and filed no fewer than five days preceding the noticed date of the hearing, unless a judge orders otherwise for good cause. (CCP section 437c(b)(4).) Petitioner's Reply papers were not filed timely, there is no good cause for the late filing presented in the Reply papers, and the Reply does not raise new matters that affect the outcome of this motion. The Court will exercise its discretion to not consider late-filed papers. (CRC, Rule 3.1300). This determination also moots the opposing party's objections and motion to strike filed on August 31, 2020. Petitioner Michael F. Hirner's Motion for Summary Judgment or in the alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication of the April 29, 2019 Will Contest is denied in part and granted in part. As explained below, triable issues of fact exist as to one or more causes of action and, therefore, Respondent is not entitled to judgment as a matter of law (CCP section 437c(c)). The motion for summary judgment is denied. Respondent's alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to menace, and otherwise denied. The motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication on the issue of decedent's testamentary capacity is denied, due to the disputed material issues of fact on each of the three statutory basis for lack of capacity: (1) sufficient mental capability at the time of execution (see Opp. to Sep. Stmnt. 42, 92-93, 95; the Narurkar's Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 108-125, 128-132, 192, 195-213, 215-226); (2) decedent's capacity to understand and recollect the nature and situation of his property at the time of execution (see Opp. to Sep. Stmnt. 3, 11, 20, 37, 39, 47, 181); (3) decedent's capacity to remember and understand his relations to living descendants, spouse, and parents, and those whose interests are affected by the will (see Opp. to Sep. Statement 4-15, 102; AMF 133-152). The motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication on the issue of undue influence as to Petitioner is denied, due to the disputed material issues of fact on this claim (see Opp. to Sep. Stmnt. 73-77, 96-102; AMF 227-240). The motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication on the issue of whether Petitioner procured the challenged testamentary instrument by duress is denied due to the disputed material issues of fact on this claim (see Opp. to Sep. Stmnt. 100; AMF 227-231). The motion in the alternative for summary adjudication of the issue of menace is granted. Petitioner carried his initial burden make a prima facie showing of the nonexistence of any triable issue of material fact as to procuring the challenged testamentary instrument by menace under Civil Code section 1570. The opposing party has not carried their burden to make their own prima facie showing of the existence of triable facts specifically as to menace under Civil Code section 1570. Menace is addressed specifically only at Opp. to Sep. Stmnt. 101, where the Narurkars dispute Petitioner's showing, but the evidence they cite does not show the elements of menace (threat of duress or violence to person, property, or character or actual violence to person, property, or character) under Civil Code section 1570. Part 1 of 2

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOK CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/30/20. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. The petition also appears to be incomplete. The caption indicates that it is a petition for letters of administration, but item 2b(1) is checked for appointment of an executor. No IAEA authority is indicated in the caption, but item 2c was checked. Item 3 was not completed. Item 6 of the petition was not completed. Not all of the parties mentioned in the will are listed in item 8. (Other documents have not yet been reviewed online, however, the proof of service appears to be incomplete.)
